person.bio: I'm a security professional passionate about technology and customer success, dedicated to architecting solutions that drive real impact. I thrive on engaging in technical discussions, helping customers navigate challenges, and showcasing innovative solutions. With a hands-on approach, I stay in the field, bridging the gap between security, technology, and business needs to ensure long-term success.

person.name: Donghwan Won
person.bio: I’m Donghwan Won, a Solution Engineer at MakinaRocks with a focus on CNCF projects and cloud-native technologies like Kubernetes, Ceph, Istio, and Prometheus. I’m particularly passionate about exploring how these technologies drive innovation in the cloud ecosystem. For more insights, best practices, and deep dives into cloud-native development, check out my tech blog at showinfo8.com.

person.name: Matthias Hauber
person.bio: Matthias Hauber is a DevOps and Cloud Engineer at PRODYNA. As part of the Managed Services team, he is responsible for the seamless operation of a large number of Kubernetes clusters and Azure environments for various customers. As a trainer for Kubernetes, Terraform and Azure, he has already been able to share his knowledge in numerous workshops.

person.name: Michael Scheef
person.bio: Just a dude trying to bend technology to his will. Not the astronaut I dreamed of becoming, but hey—it’s a start.

person.name: Stefan Luteijn
person.bio: I graduated in history and aspirations to be come a teacher. When this dream did not come to be, I found a new love in IT and open source projects. Kubernetes and everything around it quickly turned my job into a hobby. I'm currently working as a cloud engineer consultant to help customers onboard to the kubernetes platform.

person.name: Daniel Lo Man Hok
person.bio: I'm an infrastructure architect, proficient in designing scalable solutions using Infrastructure as Code (IaaC), cloud technologies, Kubernetes, and server storage platforms. I am passionate about continuously learning and architecting robust, innovative platforms, delivering a profound sense of professional accomplishment.

person.name: Hyungyu Lee
person.bio: As a Data Platform Kubernetes Engineer at LINE Plus, I specialize in orchestrating large-scale workloads for LINE's integrated messaging data pipelines. I manage a multi-tenant on-premises Kubernetes cluster consisting of 1,000+ nodes with mixed CPU and GPU resources.  I am particularly passionate about optimizing Data and AI workloads that exhibit high resource volatility to achieve peak operational efficiency. I am also eager to contribute to the CNCF ecosystem by sharing my experiences and helping advance cloud-native technologies.  Please call me just Seron :)

person.name: Jorge O. Castro
person.bio: Jorge (@castrojo) is a Developer Relations specialist at the CNCF, with a focus on improving developer experience and communities. Jorge graduated with a degree in Telecommunications from Michigan State University and rode with the 11th Armored Cavalry Regiment for four years. He has contributed to Kubernetes, Ubuntu, Kubeflow, and the Cloud Custodian open source projects. He now resides in Ann Arbor, Michigan with his lovely wife Jill, their son Rafael, and their beagle Oscar. Hobbies and interests include heavy metal, passport renewal, paleontology, gaming, technology, and scouting out the best beers in the world.

person.name: Matt Armstrong
person.bio: I started out fixing computers at a local electronics shop and since have gone into System Administration, Development, DevOps, Cloud Architect, and beyond. I love solving problems and using technology to create complex pipelines that transform highly complex issues. Please check out my blog at toledomaker.com

person.name: Dolis Sharma
person.bio: Dolis Sharma is a seasoned technologist with extensive experience guiding enterprises through their cloud-native journeys. She has deep expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, DevOps, and DevSecOps, helping organizations accelerate and scale cloud-native adoption. Dolis has built and managed mission-critical systems and successfully led multiple environments through rigorous security certifications, including SOC 2 and ISO 27001. Known for translating complex technical challenges into tangible business outcomes, she partners closely with enterprise teams, offering architecture guidance, automation strategies, and operational best practices. When she’s not exploring the world of technology, she enjoys hiking, chasing waterfalls, relaxing on the beach, or dancing. She holds a diploma in Kathak and loves moving to the rhythm of the music

person.name: Mario Lechończak
person.bio: Experienced Cloud Network Engineer specializing in AWS with a comprehensive skill set in designing, deploying, and managing sophisticated cloud network infrastructures. Passionate about continuous learning and staying ahead of emerging cloud technologies to drive innovation and efficiency

person.name: Sameer Mene
person.bio: A Cloud Solution Architect based in Pune, India, with a passion for cloud-native technologies, DevOps practices, and automation. Guided by the mantra, "If you do it more than twice, automate it!" I focus on simplifying workflows and building resilient systems. With expertise in Java, Go, and Python, I have designed and implemented solutions for Kubernetes cluster management, service mesh, operators, IAM, storage and edge computing. I specialize in crafting cloud-agnostic solutions, leveraging tools like Terraform and Pulumi to automate deployments and streamline operations across diverse environments. With a solid background in the telecom domain and a keen interest in emerging technologies such as 5G, my expertise also extends to AI, large language models (LLMs), and the dynamic landscape of Web 3.0.

person.name: Anthony Hansen
person.bio: I started with Linux and studying Cybersecurity and found my way by chance to Kubernetes. I appreciate the scope of what can be done within Kubernetes and the constant improvement and learning opportunities. I like fixing broken things.

person.name: Julien Sudan
person.bio: I am Julien Sudan, a Site Reliability Engineer with extensive experience in architecting secure, scalable cloud infrastructures and implementing DevSecOps and SRE practices. Currently working at Exoscale in Switzerland, I excel in using technologies like Kubernetes, and IaC to optimize cloud deployments across environment, whether on premise or in the Cloud. I actively contribute to the cloud-native community by sharing insights through presentations at conferences and wrote articles on different blogs. My technical proficiency and commitment to continuous learning are demonstrated by a comprehensive suite of certifications, including CISSP, Kubestronaut, and several cloud-specific credentials..
